page.title: Sotogrande vs Puerto Banus: Marina Comparison 2026

data.meta_description: Sotogrande marina vs Puerto Banus: compare facilities, dining, and boat charter access in 2026. Find which Costa del Sol port suits your yacht trip best.
data.summary: Sotogrande marina and Puerto Banus offer contrasting Costa del Sol experiences: Sotogrande is quieter and family-focused, while Puerto Banus buzzes with glamour and nightlife. This 2026 comparison covers facilities, dining, and boat charter access to help you choose.
key_facts: Puerto Banús has 915 berths; Sotogrande marina has 452 berths.
Sotogrande is 20 NM west of Puerto Banús (about 25 mins by powerboat).
Puerto Banús is 6 km from Marbella town; Sotogrande is 20 km from Estepona.
Average water temp in Marbella Bay is 22°C in August.
Sotogrande marina is part of a 4,000-acre resort with two golf courses.
Puerto Banús has over 60 restaurants; Sotogrande has about 15 within the marina.
Both marinas offer direct access to our fleet departing from Puerto Banús.

faq.q: Which marina is bigger, Sotogrande or Puerto Banus?
faq.a: Puerto Banús is larger with 915 berths and a 45,000 m² shopping area. Sotogrande marina has 452 berths but is set within a 4,000-acre resort with golf courses and a beach club. Puerto Banús feels busier and more commercial; Sotogrande is more intimate.

faq.q: Is Sotogrande marina quieter than Puerto Banus?
faq.a: Yes, Sotogrande is noticeably quieter, especially outside summer weekends. Puerto Banús is lively year-round, with peak crowds from June to September. If you prefer a relaxed evening with fewer people, Sotogrande is better.

faq.q: Can I charter a boat from Sotogrande marina?
faq.a: Our fleet departs exclusively from Puerto Banús, not Sotogrande. However, we can arrange pickup at Sotogrande for an additional fee (contact us via WhatsApp for pricing). Most guests find the 25-minute transfer from Sotogrande to Puerto Banús convenient.

faq.q: Which marina has better restaurants and nightlife?
faq.a: Puerto Banús has over 60 restaurants, including Michelin-starred options like Skina, and lively nightclubs like Pangea. Sotogrande has about 15 restaurants within the marina, more family-oriented, with a quieter bar scene. For nightlife, Puerto Banús wins.

faq.q: What are the parking facilities like at each marina?
faq.a: Puerto Banús has large underground car parks (€3–5/hour) but can be congested in summer. Sotogrande offers ample free parking close to the marina. If you're driving, Sotogrande is more convenient.

faq.q: Can I walk from Puerto Banus to Marbella town?
faq.a: It's a 6 km walk along the coastal path, taking about 1 hour 15 minutes. Many people prefer taking a taxi (€15–20) or the local bus (€1.50). Sotogrande is 20 km from Estepona, so a car or taxi is necessary.

faq.q: Which marina is better for families with children?
faq.a: Sotogrande is more family-friendly with its golf resort, tennis courts, and the Almenara beach club. Puerto Banús has a playground and kid-friendly restaurants, but the crowds and nightlife can be less suitable for young children after dark.

faq.q: Are pets allowed at Sotogrande and Puerto Banus marinas?
faq.a: Both marinas allow well-behaved dogs on leads. Puerto Banús has more pet-friendly restaurants with outdoor seating. Sotogrande's wider walkways are easier for dog walks. Always check with individual venues.

data.body_html:
Choosing between Sotogrande marina vs Puerto Banus for your Costa del Sol yacht charter in 2026? Both ports are excellent, but they serve different tastes. Puerto Banús is the glitzy, non-stop social hub with 915 berths, designer shops, and world-class dining. Sotogrande is a quieter, family-oriented resort marina with 452 berths, set within a 4,000-acre estate. This comparison covers facilities, dining, accessibility, and how each connects to our boat charter fleet departing from Puerto Banús.

Location and Access

Puerto Banús sits 6 km west of Marbella town, right on the Golden Mile. It's a 15-minute taxi from Marbella centre (€15–20). Sotogrande is 20 km west of Estepona, about 35 minutes by car from Marbella. By sea, the distance is 20 nautical miles — roughly 25 minutes in a powerboat like our Astondoa 40. Both marinas have good road access via the A-7 motorway, but Sotogrande is less congested, especially in summer.

Facilities and Berthing

Puerto Banús offers 915 berths for yachts up to 50 m, plus a 200 m commercial dock. Services include 24-hour security, fuel dock, pump-out, and a chandlery. Sotogrande has 452 berths for yachts up to 30 m, with a smaller fuel dock and fewer services. For mega-yachts, Puerto Banús is the clear choice — our Ferretti 94 (29 m) fits easily there. Sotogrande is better for smaller craft and has a more relaxed, less industrial feel.

Dining and Nightlife

Puerto Banús has over 60 restaurants, from Michelin-starred Skina to casual beachfront chiringuitos. Nightlife is legendary — Pangea and Aqwa are open until 5 am. Sotogrande has about 15 restaurants within the marina, including the excellent El Churrasco and the family-friendly La Terraza. For a quiet dinner with sunset views, Sotogrande wins. For a party, Puerto Banús is unmatched. Both have excellent seafood, but Puerto Banús has more variety.

Boat Charter Access

All our boats depart from Puerto Banús. This includes our popular Azimut 39 (11 pax) and the Mangusta 80 (12 pax) with a free jet ski. If you're staying in Sotogrande, we can arrange a taxi transfer (€45–60 one way) or a marina pickup for an additional fee — contact us via WhatsApp for details. Most guests find the 25-minute drive from Sotogrande to Puerto Banús quick and easy.

Family-Friendliness

Sotogrande is designed for families: it has a golf course, tennis courts, a beach club, and wide pedestrian walkways safe for children. Puerto Banús has a playground and kid-friendly restaurants, but the crowds and late-night noise can be overwhelming. For a family day charter, we recommend departing from Puerto Banús and heading west to Cala del Faro or Cabopino — both are quiet anchorages with shallow waters for kids.

Cost and Value

Berthing fees in Puerto Banús are higher — expect €200–400 per night for a 12 m boat in summer. Sotogrande is about 20–30% cheaper. Dining costs are comparable: a meal for two with wine is €80–150 in either marina. For charter guests, our pricing is the same regardless of marina — see our fleet rates. Tier A boats like the Astondoa 40 start at €749 for 2 hours, all inclusive.

Weather and Sea Conditions

Both marinas enjoy the same Mediterranean climate: 300+ sunny days per year, average 22°C water in August. The Strait of Gibraltar's levante wind can affect Sotogrande more than Puerto Banús, which is sheltered by the Sierra Blanca mountains. For a smoother charter, depart from Puerto Banús and head east towards Cabopino or Cala Cortés.

Frequently asked questions

Which marina is bigger, Sotogrande or Puerto Banus?

Puerto Banús is larger with 915 berths and a 45,000 m² shopping area. Sotogrande marina has 452 berths but is set within a 4,000-acre resort with golf courses and a beach club. Puerto Banús feels busier and more commercial; Sotogrande is more intimate.

Is Sotogrande marina quieter than Puerto Banus?

Yes, Sotogrande is noticeably quieter, especially outside summer weekends. Puerto Banús is lively year-round, with peak crowds from June to September. If you prefer a relaxed evening with fewer people, Sotogrande is better.

Can I charter a boat from Sotogrande marina?

Our fleet departs exclusively from Puerto Banús, not Sotogrande. However, we can arrange pickup at Sotogrande for an additional fee (contact us via WhatsApp for pricing). Most guests find the 25-minute transfer from Sotogrande to Puerto Banús convenient.

Which marina has better restaurants and nightlife?

Puerto Banús has over 60 restaurants, including Michelin-starred options like Skina, and lively nightclubs like Pangea. Sotogrande has about 15 restaurants within the marina, more family-oriented, with a quieter bar scene. For nightlife, Puerto Banús wins.

What are the parking facilities like at each marina?

Puerto Banús has large underground car parks (€3–5/hour) but can be congested in summer. Sotogrande offers ample free parking close to the marina. If you're driving, Sotogrande is more convenient.

Can I walk from Puerto Banus to Marbella town?

It's a 6 km walk along the coastal path, taking about 1 hour 15 minutes. Many people prefer taking a taxi (€15–20) or the local bus (€1.50). Sotogrande is 20 km from Estepona, so a car or taxi is necessary.

Which marina is better for families with children?

Sotogrande is more family-friendly with its golf resort, tennis courts, and the Almenara beach club. Puerto Banús has a playground and kid-friendly restaurants, but the crowds and nightlife can be less suitable for young children after dark.

Are pets allowed at Sotogrande and Puerto Banus marinas?

Both marinas allow well-behaved dogs on leads. Puerto Banús has more pet-friendly restaurants with outdoor seating. Sotogrande's wider walkways are easier for dog walks. Always check with individual venues.
